agent.name: runtime-bug-hunter
agent.role: Find runtime errors, logic bugs, null-safety violations, unhandled edge cases, dead code paths, and error handling gaps in this codebase.

coverage.incomplete_reason: Codebase contains hundreds of files; sampled critical flow and auth paths to adhere to timeframe and finding cap limits.

findings.title: Uncaught Exceptions in Supabase Auth Handlers
findings.description: Auth errors are logged to console.error but the function returns null instead of throwing or returning an Error object, potentially hiding underlying authentication failures from the caller.

proof_hooks.summary: getCurrentUser swallows auth error and returns null
Simulate network failure to observe null return instead of error
steps: Simulate a network failure during getCurrentUser()
Observe caller receiving null instead of an error state, causing opaque fallback behavior

repro_steps: Simulate a network failure during getCurrentUser()
Observe caller receiving null instead of an error state, causing opaque fallback behavior
findings.impact: Auth failures are silently swallowed; callers cannot distinguish unauthenticated state from a network error, leading to opaque fallback behavior and difficult-to-debug auth issues.

suggested_fix.approach: Return a union type `{ user: User | null, error: Error | null }` or throw the error so the caller can handle it.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: All callers of getCurrentUser must be updated to handle the new return type or caught exception.

history.notes: Discovered during static analysis of auth error-handling patterns in src/lib/supabase.ts.

findings.title: Loose Equality in Condition Node Evaluator
findings.description: The logic evaluator uses loose equality (`==` and `!=`) for the 'equals' and 'notEquals' operators. This can lead to unexpected type coercion bugs (e.g. `0 == "0"`, `false == 0`, `"" == false`).

proof_hooks.summary: Loose equality operators used in condition evaluator
Create condition with mixed-type comparison to observe unexpected true result
steps: Create a condition node in a flow
Compare the boolean false with integer 0 or empty string ""

repro_steps: Create a condition node in a flow
Compare the boolean false with integer 0 or empty string ""
findings.impact: Flow conditions may evaluate incorrectly when comparing values of different types, causing flows to take wrong branches silently.

suggested_fix.approach: Change `left == right` to `left === right` and explicitly cast types if type coercion is the intended behavior (e.g. `String(left) === String(right)`).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Existing flows that relied on loose equality for cross-type comparisons will change behavior. Audit saved flows before deploying.

history.notes: Discovered during static analysis of condition node evaluation logic.

findings.title: Missing Code/State Check on Auth Callback OAuth Exchange
findings.description: The callback handler calls `supabase.auth.getSession()` unconditionally if `error` is not in the URL, under the assumption Supabase automatically traded the code. If the code parameter is missing or invalid, it may silently get an unauthenticated session and redirect the user back to login with no explanation.

proof_hooks.summary: getSession called unconditionally without checking for code param
Navigate to callback without query params to observe silent redirect
steps: Navigate to /auth/callback with no query parameters
Observe redirection to /login without any error message or alert

repro_steps: Navigate to /auth/callback with no query parameters
Observe redirection to /login without any error message or alert
findings.impact: Users arriving at the OAuth callback URL without a valid code parameter are silently redirected to login with no explanation, degrading the sign-in UX and making OAuth errors hard to diagnose.

suggested_fix.approach: Look for the `code` query parameter and surface an 'Invalid Link' error if it is missing, before attempting session exchange or redirecting silently.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk; only adds a guard before the existing getSession call.

history.notes: Inferred from reading AuthCallback.tsx handleCallback logic; no runtime evidence available.

findings.title: Batch Task Creation Does Not Wait for Errors
findings.description: When `handleCreateAll` invokes `onClose()`, the component may unmount before the analytics event is fully processed or before state settles. Although `onBatchCreate` is awaited, `setIsCreating(false)` in the finally block will execute on an unmounted component, potentially leading to a React state warning.

proof_hooks.summary: setIsCreating called in finally block after onClose may trigger on unmounted component
Slow network click to observe React unmounted component warning
steps: Observe React 'Cannot update state on an unmounted component' warning in console

repro_steps: Observe React 'Cannot update state on an unmounted component' warning in console
findings.impact: React console warning on slow networks; no user-visible data loss but may indicate a memory leak pattern if component is frequently mounted/unmounted.

suggested_fix.approach: Check if component is still mounted before calling `setIsCreating(false)`, or allow the modal to close itself naturally by letting the parent remove it from the DOM based on state.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Very low risk; only adds a mounted ref guard.

history.notes: Inferred from reading SuggestionsModal.tsx handleCreateAll async flow pattern.

next_actions.action: Refactor src/lib/supabase.ts to throw specific Auth errors to callers instead of returning null.
next_actions.rationale: Auth error transparency is critical for diagnosing login failures; P1 severity justifies early fix.
Condition node is core flow logic; type coercion bugs silently corrupt flow evaluation.

next_actions.action: Add code check in AuthCallback.tsx before calling getSession.
next_actions.rationale: Trivial guard that prevents silent OAuth callback failures and improves auth UX.
